title: SketchArcs Object
description: The collection of arcs in a sketch. This provides access to the existing arcs and supports the methods to create new arcs.

methods.name: addByCenterStartEnd
methods.description: Creates a sketch arc that is centered at the specified point and between the two input points.

methods.name: addByCenterStartSweep
methods.description: Creates a sketch arc that is always parallel to the x-y plane of the sketch and is centered at the specified point.

methods.name: addByThreePoints
methods.description: Creates a sketch arc that passes through the three points.

methods.name: addFillet
methods.description: Creates a fillet between two sketch entities The side (quadrant) the fillet is created on is determined by the points specified. The point for each entity can be its startSketchPoint or endSketchPoint

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch arc using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of arcs in the sketch.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Sketch fillet and offset API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the creation of a fillet in a sketch and offset a set of curves.

addByCenterStartEnd.title: SketchArcs.addByCenterStartEnd Method
addByCenterStartEnd.description: Creates a sketch arc that is centered at the specified point and between the two input points. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArcs_var.addByCenterStartEnd(centerPoint, startPoint, endPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArcs_var.addByCenterStartEnd(centerPoint, startPoint, endPoint, normal)

addByCenterStartEnd.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchArc or null in the case of a failure.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: The center point of the arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The end point of the arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object. If the end point does not lie on the arc, a new point would be created such that it lies on the arc.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.description: An optional argument that specifies the normal of the plane the arc will be created on. If not supplied, a vector in the positive Z direction will be used, which results in the creation of an arc that is parallel to the X-Y plane of the sketch. However, you can specify a normal vector to orient the arc in any orientation. The normal also helps to control the sweep direction of the arc, where the sweep direction is always counterclockwise from the start to the end point, where counterclockwise is defined using the right-hand rule around the normal vector.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

addByCenterStartSweep.title: SketchArcs.addByCenterStartSweep Method
addByCenterStartSweep.description: Creates a sketch arc that is always parallel to the x-y plane of the sketch and is centered at the specified point. Remarks
addByCenterStartSweep.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchArc object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: The center point of the arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the arc. The distance between this point and the center defines the radius of the arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: sweepAngle
parameters.description: The sweep of the arc. This is defined in radians and a positive value defines a counter-clockwise sweep.

addByThreePoints.title: SketchArcs.addByThreePoints Method
addByThreePoints.description: Creates a sketch arc that passes through the three points. Remarks
addByThreePoints.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchArc or null in the case of a failure.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: A point along the arc. This is a Point3D object.

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The end point of the arc. This can be either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object.

addFillet.title: SketchArcs.addFillet Method
addFillet.description: Creates a fillet between two sketch entities The side (quadrant) the fillet is created on is determined by the points specified. The point for each entity can be its startSketchPoint or endSketchPoint

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchArcs_var.addFillet(firstEntity, firstEntityPoint, secondEnitity, secondEntityPoint, radius)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchArcs_var->addFillet(firstEntity, firstEntityPoint, secondEnitity, secondEntityPoint, radius);

addFillet.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchArc object (fillet) if the operation was successful or null if it failed.

parameters.name: firstEntity
parameters.description: The first curve for the fillet definition. The curve must be open.

parameters.name: firstEntityPoint
parameters.description: A point on or closer to one end of the first curve that indicates the side to create the fillet on

parameters.name: secondEnitity
parameters.description: The second curve for the fillet definition. The curve must be open.

parameters.name: secondEntityPoint
parameters.description: A point on or closer to one end of the second curve that indicates the side to create the fillet on

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: radius of the arc in centimeters

related_samples.name: Sketch fillet and offset API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the creation of a fillet in a sketch and offset a set of curves.

classType.title: SketchArcs.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: SketchArcs.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch arc using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: SketchArcs.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of arcs in the sketch.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchArcs_var.count

isValid.title: SketchArcs.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchArcs_var.isValid

objectType.title: SketchArcs.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchArcs_var.objectType
